reasoning:
The target threshold is $96.78, while the current stock price is higher at $98.09. This gives a buffer of roughly 1.35%. Under standard assumptions of a random walk with variance scaling with time, the probability of finishing above a slightly-lower threshold shrinks toward 50% as the forecast horizon extends, because expanding variance eventually dwarfs the initial small buffer. 

For January 11 (one week out), the $1.31 buffer represents approximately 0.3 to 0.4 standard deviations. Assuming mostly calm trading, the probability of remaining positive is fairly solid at ~63%.

For February 3 (one month out), structural volatility steps in. Past Q4 earnings reports were typically held on the final days of January or the first days of February (e.g., Jan 31 in 2025, Feb 1 in 2024). This adds large jump risk to the stock exactly around the February 3 resolution date, rapidly expanding the variance and eroding the predictive power of our initial buffer. The probability correspondingly draws down to ~56%.

For April 4 (three months out), the time horizon allows a large span of normal price movement, earnings guidance digestion, and broader market variations. The current $1.3% cushion becomes almost negligible against ~3 months of stock volatility (~15% annualized standard deviation over an expected quarter). As such, and guided by the instruction that uncertainty should increase with the forecast horizon, I bring the April probability close to the base rate at ~52%, reflecting near-complete diffusion of the current mild advantage.
